ideas.title: Agent-based modeling of lifestyle intervention adoption in cancer care systems
ideas.description: This research employs agent-based modeling to simulate a healthcare system comprising patients, oncologists, primary care providers, hospitals, and insurers. The core objective is to model the adoption and integration of adjunctive lifestyle programs into standard cancer therapy protocols and to predict the system-wide effects on treatment adherence and clinical outcomes. By simulating different implementation strategies (e.g., top-down policy mandates vs. grassroots professional advocacy) and individual agent behaviors (e.g., patient trust, clinician time constraints), the model can identify scenarios where lifestyle support synergistically improves therapy adherence versus where it might inadvertently create competing demands or inequitable access. This provides a predictive tool for designing implementation pathways that maximize benefit without detracting from essential care.

ideas.title: Overcoming Structural Blockades: Measuring the True Teachable Moment When Barriers Are Removed
ideas.description: This study investigates whether a cancer diagnosis creates a universal 'teachable moment' for health behavior change when structural and resource-based barriers are actively mitigated. Researchers will first catalog the specific practical obstacles (financial, logistical, social, systemic) faced by a diverse cohort of new survivors. Participants are then randomized to receive either standard care or a tailored intervention package designed to remove their identified primary barriers. Subsequent measurement of health behavior change intentions and actions across demographic and psychological subgroups will reveal if equitable receptivity emerges once external blockades are reduced, providing a more robust test of the teachable moment hypothesis.

ideas.title: A closed-loop, context-aware reinforcement learning system for adaptive digital health nudges
ideas.description: This redesigned system moves beyond static nudges to a dynamic, learning intervention. It continuously gathers behavioral and contextual data from smartphone sensors and user feedback to model an individual's state and habit strength. A reinforcement learning agent uses this model to optimize the delivery of behavioral nudges, deciding not just what message to send, but whether, when, and how to intervene—or to pause interventions to avoid habituation. The goal is to sustain engagement by making support precisely responsive to the survivor's lived experience, thereby improving the durability of lifestyle changes.

ideas.title: Adapt Environmental Nudge Architecture to Digital Health for Sustained Behavior Change
ideas.description: This idea adapts solutions from environmental psychology and sustainable design, where 'choice architecture' is used to encourage pro-environmental behaviors by structuring the physical environment to make sustainable choices easier and more salient. The analogous solution involves reducing friction and embedding cues directly into the user's surroundings, such as making stairs more attractive than elevators. Adapted to digital health for survivors, this translates to moving beyond standard push notifications to architect the survivor's entire digital-physical interface for sustained lifestyle change. The research would design the smartphone interface (e.g., a default health dashboard) and integrate tangible, context-aware cues in the physical environment (e.g., a connected pill bottle placed on the coffee machine) to make healthy behaviors the default, low-friction path, thereby leveraging principles of behavioral economics through embedded environmental design rather than just digital reminders.

ideas.title: Adversarially Robust Nudge Design via Multi-Agent Simulation of Behavioral Market Dynamics
ideas.description: This research creates a simulated marketplace to stress-test behavioral nudges against active commercial countermeasures. Using an agent-based model, it pits public health interventions against adaptive algorithms representing food industry marketing strategies, which seek to maximize sales of high-trans-fat products. The system iteratively generates and selects nudge designs that prove most resilient to this adversarial pressure. This produces a portfolio of robust, empirically vetted choice architecture strategies with a higher likelihood of sustained real-world impact.

ideas.title: Adapting Masterclass and Workshop Models from Professional Education to Survivor Lifestyle Programs
ideas.description: This idea adapts solution models from professional and continuing education to the context of lifestyle education for cancer survivors. The analogous problem in fields like business or technology is efficiently delivering advanced, applicable skill development to a motivated cohort. The solution there is the expert-led 'masterclass' or intensive workshop, which combines authoritative instruction with structured peer collaboration and community building. Adapted to this research context, we propose developing and testing 'Survivor Wellness Masterclass' models. These are efficient, topic-specific group visits that go beyond simple education delivery. Led by a clinical specialist, they would incorporate core masterclass elements: direct expert teaching, facilitated peer-to-peer problem-solving based on shared survivor experiences, small-group goal-setting activities, and deliberate cultivation of a learning cohort that provides mutual support and accountability after the session concludes, thereby enhancing long-term lifestyle modification.

ideas.title: Adapting Microfluidic Organ-on-a-Chip Technology to Study TFA-Induced Organelle Dysfunction
ideas.description: Drawing an analogy from toxicology and environmental engineering, where microfluidic organ-on-a-chip devices are used to study the effects of pollutants on specific human tissues with high physiological relevance and resolution. The analogous solution involves culturing human cells in engineered microenvironments to observe real-time toxicant effects. This solution is adapted to the TFA research context by developing a dedicated microfluidic platform for hepatocytes or cardiomyocytes that integrates biosensors for mitochondrial membrane potential, ROS production, and ER calcium homeostasis. The adapted idea is to use this 'organelle stress-on-a-chip' model to expose cells to physiological and pathological concentrations of specific TFAs (like elaidic acid) and monitor the spatiotemporal dynamics of organelle dysfunction, cross-talk, and the onset of stress signaling pathways in a controlled, scalable system that bridges cellular and sub-cellular analysis.

ideas.title: Clinical Triage for Equitable Community Pathway Activation
ideas.description: This redesigned system uses clinical settings not in a supporting role, but as an active equity-focused triage and navigation hub. It identifies high-risk, isolated, or complex patients through clinical data and then systematically connects them to appropriate, vetted community resources with dedicated support. Community health navigators, embedded in clinics, bridge the gap by facilitating initial engagement and providing follow-up. The model ensures community strategies are powerfully leveraged, but their reach and effectiveness are primary managed and monitored by a clinical backbone to prevent the exclusion of the most vulnerable.
